task_objective: Ensure a deliberate QAM game-dropdown choice beats the next Steam-context auto-selection arm without disabling running-game auto-selection on a genuine later QAM reopen.
tests_added: Decision-function tests prove an explicit selection consumes a visible pending arm instead of selecting, remains pending while hidden, and takes precedence over operation-in-progress handling.
Existing decision cases now explicitly prove that false explicitSelectionPending preserves their prior wait, consume, and select outcomes.
The useSteamContext regression proves an explicit selection does not call the displayed-game setter and invokes onExplicitSelectionConsumed exactly once.

The review-round regression defers the selection effect, changes the ref-backed value after the hook call, and proves the effect consumes false-to-true state while selecting the running game for true-to-false state.
design_decisions: The fix uses the plan-mandated one-shot ref signal with no timer or QAM-close duration heuristic.
resolveQamOpenSelection checks explicitSelectionPending after the existing wait guard and before operationInProgress, so a hidden panel never consumes the flag.
The dropdown wrapper sets explicitSelectionRef before delegating to onGameChange, and useSteamContext clears the flag only when it consumes the pending auto-selection arm.

The applyCachedRefreshResult and applyRefreshResult Steam-context selection paths were considered and deliberately excluded. Their allowSteamContextSelection=true callers are initial-load paths, not dropdown interactions, so both call sites remain unchanged.

Known limitation accepted by the plan: if a future Steam build stops changing QAM visibility when the dropdown opens, the one-shot flag would be consumed by the next genuine reopen and one running-game auto-selection would be missed. No timeout was added.
No backend, dependency, GameSettingsSection, or refresh-path behavior changed.

The review-round fix passes isExplicitSelectionPending as a getter and reads it once inside the selection effect. This avoids depending on a non-reactive render-time ref snapshot or incidental callback identity changes.

results.tdd: The initial focused red run failed because explicit selection still resolved to select and setDisplayedGame was called. In the review round, the deferred-effect regression failed because the snapshot-based API selected Running Game after the ref-backed value changed; after the getter implementation, both focused test files passed all 12 tests.
results.automated: The behavior-commit hook passed Ruff, formatting, ty, 839 Python tests at 88.69% coverage, frontend supply-chain checks, build, TypeScript typecheck, and 317 frontend tests. The orchestration quality gate is run once more after this documentation commit before the round-complete marker is written.
results.scope: Frontend-only; no py_modules backend file changed, GameSettingsSection.tsx was untouched, and both refresh-path selectCurrentSteamGameIfAvailable call sites remain unchanged.
results.release: No release, tag, push, or manual merge is part of this implementation round; approved orchestration finalization owns those actions.
results.on_device: Deferred and not claimed as passed. With a game running, Deck verification must confirm repeated dropdown choices remain displayed without qam_context auto-selection overwrite logs while settings_change_persisted still reports each choice; a full close/reopen must then auto-select the running game. With no game running, dropdown choices must still persist across close/reopen. Logs should be pulled with ./run.sh uv run python scripts/pull_plugin_logs.py --host steamdeck-legos.
